title: Node.js Intro
content:
Node.js Introduction

What is Node.js?

Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome V8 JavaScript engine. It allows you to run JavaScript on the server side.

Why Use Node.js?

  • Fast performance - V8 engine
  • JavaScript everywhere - full-stack
  • Rich ecosystem - npm packages

title: Node.js Installation
content:
Node.js Installation

Installing Node.js

Download Node.js from nodejs.org. Choose the LTS version.

Check Versions
node --version
npm --version

title: Node.js First App
content:
Your First Node.js App

hello.js
console.log("Hello, World!");

Terminal
node hello.js

title: Node.js Modules
content:
Node.js Modules

Built-in Modules

Importing Modules
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');
const http = require('http');
console.log("Modules imported!");

Custom Modules

myModule.js
module.exports = {
    greet: function(name) {
        return "Hello, " + name;
    },
    add: function(a, b) {
        return a + b;
    }
};

title: Node.js File System
content:
Node.js File System

Reading Files

Read File
const fs = require('fs');
var data = fs.readFileSync('file.txt', 'utf8');
console.log(data);

Writing Files

Write File
const fs = require('fs');
fs.writeFileSync('newfile.txt', 'Hello Node!');

title: Node.js HTTP Server
content:
Node.js HTTP Server

Basic Server
const http = require('http');
var server = http.createServer(function(req, res) {
    res.writeHead(200, {'Content-Type': 'text/html'});
    res.end('
Hello Node!
');
});
server.listen(3000);

JSON API

JSON Response
var data = {name: 'John', age: 30};
res.end(JSON.stringify(data));

title: Node.js NPM
content:
Node.js NPM

Installing Packages

Commands
npm init
npm install express
npm install --save-dev nodemon

package.json

package.json
{
  "name": "my-app",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.18.2"
  }
}

title: Node.js Express
content:
Node.js Express

Install
npm install express
Server
const express = require('express');
var app = express();
app.get('/', function(req, res) {
    res.send('
Hello Express!
');
});
app.listen(3000);

Routes

REST Routes
app.get('/api/users', function(req, res) {
    res.json({users: ['John', 'Jane']});
});
app.post('/api/users', function(req, res) {
    res.json({message: 'Created'});
});

title: Node.js Middleware
content:
Node.js Middleware

What is Middleware?

Middleware functions have access to request and response objects.

Example
var logger = function(req, res, next) {
    console.log(req.method, req.url);
    next();
};
app.use(logger);

Body Parser

JSON
app.use(express.json());
app.post('/api/data', function(req, res) {
    console.log(req.body);
    res.json({received: req.body});
});

title: Node.js MongoDB
content:
Node.js MongoDB

Install
npm install mongoose
Connect
const mongoose = require('mongoose');
mongoose.connect('mongodb://localhost:27017/mydb')
    .then(function() { console.log('Connected'); });

Schema and Model

Example
var userSchema = new mongoose.Schema({
    name: String,
    email: String
});
var User = mongoose.model('User', userSchema);

title: Node.js REST API
content:
Node.js REST API

Complete REST API

API
var express = require('express');
var app = express();
app.use(express.json());
var users = [{id: 1, name: 'John'}];
app.get('/api/users', function(req, res) {
    res.json(users);
});
app.post('/api/users', function(req, res) {
    var newUser = {id: users.length + 1, name: req.body.name};
    users.push(newUser);
    res.status(201).json(newUser);
});
app.listen(3000);

title: Node.js Async Basics
content:
Node.js Async

Callbacks

Callback
fs.readFile('file.txt', function(err, data) {
    if (err) throw err;
    console.log(data);
});

Promises

Promise
var promise = new Promise(function(resolve, reject) {
    resolve('Success!');
});
promise.then(function(result) { console.log(result); });

Async/Await

Async
async function fetchData() {
    try {
        var result = await someAsyncCall();
        console.log(result);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(err);
    }
}

title: Node.js Events
content:
Node.js Events

Event Emitter

Example
var EventEmitter = require('events').EventEmitter;
var emitter = new EventEmitter();
emitter.on('event', function() {
    console.log('Event triggered!');
});
emitter.emit('event');

HTTP Server Events

Server
server.on('request', function(req, res) {
    console.log('Request received');
});
server.on('connection', function(socket) {
    console.log('New connection');
});

title: Node.js Streams
content:
Node.js Streams

Reading Streams

Read Stream
var fs = require('fs');
var readStream = fs.createReadStream('file.txt');
readStream.on('data', function(chunk) {
    console.log(chunk);
});
readStream.on('end', function() {
    console.log('Done');
});

Piping

Pipe
var readStream = fs.createReadStream('input.txt');
var writeStream = fs.createWriteStream('output.txt');
readStream.pipe(writeStream);

title: Node.js Error Handling
content:
Node.js Error Handling

Try-Catch

Sync
try {
    var data = JSON.parse(invalidJSON);
} catch (err) {
    console.error(err.message);
}

Async Errors

Async
promise
    .then(function(data) { console.log(data); })
    .catch(function(err) { console.error(err); });

Express Error Handler

Middleware
app.use(function(err, req, res, next) {
    console.error(err.stack);
    res.status(500).json({error: 'Error'});
});

title: Node.js Authentication
content:
Node.js Authentication

Install
npm install jsonwebtoken bcryptjs

JWT Token

Generate
var jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
var token = jwt.sign(
    {id: user.id},
    'secret_key',
    {expiresIn: '1h'}
);

Middleware

Auth
function authenticateToken(req, res, next) {
    var token = req.headers['authorization'];
    if (!token) return res.status(401);
    jwt.verify(token, 'secret', function(err, user) {
        if (err) return res.status(403);
        req.user = user;
        next();
    });
}

title: Node.js Best Practices
content:
Node.js Best Practices

Project Structure

  • Use clear folder structure
  • Separate concerns (MVC)
  • Use environment variables

Error Handling

Best Practice
process.on('uncaughtException', function(err) {
    console.error(err);
    process.exit(1);
});

Security

Tips
var helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet());
var cors = require('cors');
app.use(cors());

title: Node.js Testing
content:
Node.js Testing

Install Jest
npm install --save-dev jest

Unit Test

Test
test('adds numbers', function() {
    expect(add(2, 3)).toBe(5);
});
function add(a, b) { return a + b; }

API Test

Supertest
var request = require('supertest');
request(app)
    .get('/api/users')
    .expect(200);

npm install --save-dev supertest

title: Node.js Deployment
content:
Node.js Deployment

PM2 Process Manager

PM2
npm install -g pm2
pm2 start app.js
pm2 list
pm2 restart app.js
pm2 logs

Environment Variables

.env
PORT=3000
DB_HOST=localhost
JWT_SECRET=secret
NODE_ENV=production

Docker

Dockerfile
FROM node:18-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

title: Node.js Summary
content:
Node.js Course Summary

You Did It!

Congratulations on completing the Node.js course!

  • Basics - Modules, File System, HTTP
  • Express - Server, Routing, Middleware
  • Database - MongoDB, Mongoose
  • APIs - RESTful APIs
  • Advanced - Async, Events, Streams
  • Production - Testing, Deployment

Next Steps

  • Build a REST API with Express
  • Create real-time apps with Socket.io
  • Learn GraphQL
  • Explore TypeScript

Keep coding with Node.js!
